description_long: This object is a linear arrangement of roughly hewn stone blocks, partially embedded in the ground and surrounded by patches of green moss and small plants. The stones are irregular in shape and size, forming a slightly curved, stepped path or barrier. The surface of the stones is weathered, with visible rough textures and some moss growth, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. A single upright stick or post protrudes from the middle section, possibly serving as a marker or remnant of a former structure. The stones are closely packed, with minimal visible gaps, and the overall structure appears solid and heavy. The presence of vegetation and the natural, unpolished appearance suggest it is part of an outdoor landscape feature, such as a garden path or park edging.
description: A curved row of weathered stone blocks, partially covered with moss and small plants, forming a rugged outdoor barrier or path.
description_view_0: From this angle, the stone blocks appear elongated and low, with moss and greenery visible along the top and sides.
description_view_1: This view highlights the curvature of the stone arrangement and shows more of the moss and plant growth along the edge.
description_view_2: Looking from above, the stepped arrangement of the stones is clear, with the rough texture and vegetation more prominent.
description_view_3: This perspective emphasizes the uneven, stepped surface of the stones and the upright stick or post in the center.
attribute_location_context: garden landscaping or park pathway edging
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked and partially embedded in soil
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible moss growth on edges and surfaces, with rough, weathered stone textures indicating long-term outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: interlocked with neighboring blocks and integrated with surrounding soil and vegetation
